name: Please, Don't Touch Anything: House Broken

reviews.review_title: Great sequel to a great game
reviews.review_description: The Mixed reality is so smooth and the endings are awesome

I LOVED part 1 & can't wait to get this one to work.  Unfortunately AR mode, rather than simply turning on the cameras for the ability to see your real-life background, seems to look for a pre-scanned "room" even if that's nowhere near where the player actually is :(  This leads to seeing the menu/game 50 to 100 feet away from whatever my current location is, even if I scan that into another stored room.  Otherwise it'd be the perfect racquet game; for now I'm stuck using the VR mode though.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you didn't have the best experience. If you'd like to drop us a line at support@forwardxp.com we can look into this further with you.

reviews.review_title: Not playable anymore with my room setup
reviews.review_description: Somehow, the objects i placed in my room to get a better experience in mixed reality won't be recognized anymore, like a table or bench. I hope they can fix this.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you didn't have the best experience. If you'd like to drop us a line at support@forwardxp.com we can look into this further with you.

reviews.review_description: Good puzzle game. If you enjoyed the original Please, Don't Touch Anything, you'll likely have fun with this one too. It makes good use of mixed reality, with some really cool effects that enhance the experience. The main downside is that it's fairly short. I wish there were more endings and content to explore. Still, I definitely recommend it if you're into puzzles or enjoy MR features.

I bought this game, and was really really looking forward to it after beating the first in VR. Unfortunately, even after clearing out a lot of space in my room to even have enough room to begin with, there was no way to adjust the height. After trying, looking up solutions and problems other people have had, there just doesn't seem to be a way to adjust it. This, I'm refunding the game to get my money back for what could easily be solved was just a way to adjust height

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you're not having the best experience in VR. Could you please drop us a line at support@forwardxp.com so that we can look into this further with you?

reviews.review_title: Great sequel, Great gam, Just short
reviews.review_description: I absolutely loved the first game and have replayed it countless times on here, and this one has the fun of the original. Only complaint is that there are fewer endings, so once you get the hang of the first few, the rest of the game can be pretty quick. I was able to finish the game in just about 2 hours, while the first game took me a few good days. Other than that, amazing game!
The puzzles in this game are really good, and it I'd a must-have.I am also really excited for alien rogue incursion.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words! We're so glad you had fun!

reviews.review_description: Not sure about this concept where we need to use all clues for explicit dead ends before doing THE sequence leading to success. There is no logical and without any clue from the game when we are blocked, it's almost impossible to guess what to fo. Some good ideas though but I prefere some real escape game where some logic leads us to do some actions from A to Z to complete the mission.
I loved the first one and this one did not disappoint.

developer_response.body: We're so glad to hear that you enjoyed it! Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_title: A must have for puzzle lovers!
reviews.review_description:
This game, much like the original, are absolute staples in vr puzzle games! What seems to be a simple concept of pressing a button, and things happening in response, very quickly becomes more and more interesting. From solving logic puzzles, to observing small details, this game has  so much variety in the puzzles you solve! It may get confusing from time to time, and you may get stuck, but when you past the one puzzle that you couldn’t get past, you feel like an absolute genius!

Graphics wise, the game has a simple style that is quite charming, and won’t make you lose focus one you puzzle solving. The mixed reality, while not my favorite (i’m not a huge fan of mr in general) looks amazing, even with the simple graphics!

I myself have not gotten all the endings, but i have gotten some rather conplicated ones, such as Spaghetti! A tip i have, is that everything in this game has a purpose. You might not understand it at first, but know that if it’s included, it has at least 1 use.

While the game is smaller than the original, the mechanics are improved, and the Mixed Reality is nice, even if it’s not my cup of tea.

All in all, this is a must have for any and all VR puzzle lovers, like me.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the thoughtful feedback, we're glad you've had such a great time! Happy puzzling!

reviews.review_description: Great game. I mean it has puzzles, mistory and horror. Just a little confusing. Overall good job Dev team 👍

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing! We're happy you liked it!

reviews.review_description: IT HAS HAND TRACKING AND REALLY GOOD ENDING AND YOU CAN PLAY IN YOUR ROOM

developer_response.body: We're so glad you had a great time! Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description: Classic please don't touch anything experience delivered straight to your room, really recommend this as a starting title for MR if you like solving puzzles

reviews.review_description:
after a while of playing the game, it flipped upside down and above my roof when i switched to mixed reality. i can no longer select anything from the menu to change it back because. i’ve uninstalled, restarted, and reinstalled on my headset twice now but the game always loads in the same place. i’ve also tried to remap my play area but no success. if there is a fix to this please let me know because, besides this bug, the game is 

EDIT: i’m not sure what changed, but the game is back to normal. maybe the devs pushed an update through and i didn’t notice. will update the review to 5 stars!

Can you please email us at support@forwardxp.com with what device you're playing on and any other information? We're confident we can get this taken care of for you.

reviews.review_description: The game would always shift the desk up a metre and there was no way to change height. Tried to fix it for over an hour and nothing worked. This is a buggy mess

developer_response.body: We're really sorry to hear that you're experiencing this issue, and we want to help you get it resolved and back into the game. Can you please email us at support@forwardxp.com with your device, and whether this is happening in MR/VR, or both? We'd love to get your issue resolved!

reviews.review_description: If you're into puzzle solving and spending time to figure out clues this is such a great game. I've spent quite a lot of time in the game now and still have quite a few endings yet to solve. It's definitely worth the price for how much you get out of it!

developer_response.body: We're so glad you've enjoyed it, happy puzzling!

reviews.review_description: I love this game, but I don't understand why there isn't a startup menu. When I open the game, it automatically resumes from where I left off. I'd love to let my friend's have a go on it, but there doesn't seem to be a way to allow other people to experience the game themselves, starting it from the very beginning as I did, (you know, with zero stickers) without it affecting my own progress! Do I have to complete the entire game before somebody else can play it? Or do I just have to let them play and solve my puzzles for me?  :-( Also, a 'Hint' feature option would be nice to have when you hit an absolute stumping point.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback!
You're correct in that there are no other player profiles available in the game, but this is a great idea! At the time, Housebroken is designed as a single player experience, so we're happy to pass this along to our development team as well as your idea on the hint system!

reviews.review_description: This game is great! I was a huge fan of the first one, and this one is better! Well maybe better because I miss this kind of game, anyways I'm very confident that I'll be pulling an all nighter playing this game.

developer_response.body: We're so glad to hear we hit the mark! Thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description: Finger tracking! Mixed Reality! And new puzzles! This game met every aspects of my expectation for a Don’t touch anything sequel. It’s so much fun to play this game. It’s a really good puzzle game, and showcased quest3’s Mixed Reality potential.

developer_response.body: We love hearing how much fun you had! Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_title: This game is hard, but so interresting
reviews.review_description: please do not touch anything is a puzzle game. If you were into breaking rules and puzzle solving this game is really for you please do not touch anything is a game where you get a new consul, and you were told to not touch anything but you can break the rules a little bit and touch some stuff when I first got this game. I did what I was told and did not touch anything but when I restart, I did the opposite of what I've been told. I have touch stuff pushed stuff and I've even set a nuke off in the city and got abducted by aliens like I said before if you were into puzzle solving and breaking rules, this game is for you.

developer_response.body: We're happy to hear you decided to go against the suggestion and touch all the things! Happy destruction!

reviews.review_title: So fun, except that it’s glitchy kinda
reviews.review_description:
So funny, it’s just kind of glitchy kind of a little bi,,,,,,,gituviu,bud,it’d,yrs,utctd,had run rabbit run rarararbbbiit
 please
What a perfect sequel to the first. It combines all the best new technology and the perfect difficulty of puzzles to give you the perfect game. And for only 10 bucks it is a steal.

developer_response.body: We're happy you enjoyed it! Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description: This game is as challenging as it is fun, the more you solve, the more options there will be, just like the first game, but with a new setting and a whole new set of puzzles, it’s got a lot more to explore

developer_response.body: We're so glad you're enjoying it! Thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description: This is not a PDTA 2, it's more of a mixed reality experiment. Modules feel more single purpose, along with half as many, rather straightforward endings, some reused. It's alright for what it is, but I'd recommend the original over this.

reviews.review_title: Arbitrary puzzle design. Dull and short.
reviews.review_description: If you like to randomly press buttons until something new happens, then maybe this is for you, but I found it dull and frustrating. There is very little content, and it's very basic. Once you find a new puzzle (by randomly pressing buttons), it then becomes a bit more logical and deductible, but also quite easy.

reviews.review_title: Great sequel to an amazing game
reviews.review_description: I’ve always loved the first game (2D version) and the first VR game and this one is pretty good also. I like seeing ending references to the original game and how some of them have been spiced up bit, and the new setting and hints are really cool but the only caveat I have personally with the game is the that getting the final code to await authorization is hard and does not have even the slightest of hints on how to progress. Other than that I would recommend this for anyone who’s played/seen the original or VR version.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing! We're glad to hear you liked it!

reviews.review_title: There is some parts to repair
reviews.review_description: That what is working is cool, however trying to put numbers you want into phone is really hard to achieve-even for 90's child. I will see if some updates will make it more user -friendly.
The game is amazing! But the puzzles in it are a little too hard for me over all it is a verily good game just too hard in my opinion
